title: roegneria ciliaris subsp. amurensis
intro: Roegneria ciliaris subsp. amurensis, also known as Korean reed grass or amur reed grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. Roegneria ciliaris subsp. amurensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to treat various respiratory and infectious diseases in traditional Eastern medicine.
